claim: Adaptive context routing to specialist heads improves pass@1 on long-horizon research QA.
rationale: Routing should reduce irrelevant retrieval and improve focused evidence use under fixed context windows.
intervention: mixture-of-experts router + per-head retrieval top_k in {4,8,12}

claim: Progress-aware reward shaping increases multi-step task completion for tool-using agents.
rationale: Promise/progress signals should improve credit assignment compared to sparse terminal rewards.
intervention: dense reward model with promise/progress labels + GAE smoothing

claim: Latency-conditioned model routing improves utility per wall-clock under tool latency.
rationale: Small models should dominate fast-feedback loops while larger models win in long-wait planning segments.

claim: Grounded citation validation reduces hallucinated claims in weekly briefs.
rationale: Adding citation consistency checks should lower unsupported statements without major recall loss.

claim: Malformed hypothesis records should fail closed with explicit validation errors.
